stimulus: At twelve articles long, the preamble to the constitution of ______blank country in South America—is far longer than the brief preamble to the Constitution of Canada.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of paired dashes to set off a supplementary appositive. Because the sentence already contains a closing dash before "is far longer," an opening dash is needed before the appositive "a country in South America" to form a matched pair.

Choice A is incorrect because a colon cannot pair with the closing dash that follows. Choice B is incorrect because a comma cannot pair with a closing dash. Choice D is incorrect because the appositive must be set off with some kind of punctuation.
